titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the John Masefield Collection
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday August 7, 2018 at 21:03
Transformed with yale.addEadidUrl.xsl. Adds @url with handle for finding aid. Overwrites @url if already present.
beinecke.mase.xml converted for compliance with Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines with brbl-migrate-01.xsl (mr2007-08-13).
PUBLIC "-//Yale University::Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library//TEXT (US::CtYBR::::[JOHN MASEFIELD COLLECTION ])//EN" "mase.xml" converted from EAD 1.0 to 2002 by v1to02.xsl (sy2003-10-15).

did.unittitle: John Masefield collection

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Contains letters from Masefield to various people, including Florence Hardy (mainly about her husband, Thomas Hardy), Joyce King, Elkin Mathews, and C. K. Ogden; manuscripts of poems by Masefield; photographs of Masefield; and articles about him.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Ongoing collection of documents acquired by gift and purchase from various sources. Type of accession (gift or purchase) and date of acquisition is noted in the box-and-folder list. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The John Masefield Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: John Masefield Collection. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Box 3 contains Restricted fragile material. Reference surrogates have been substituted in the main files. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This finding aid was produced from previously existing cards in the Manuscripts Catalog, or from another inventory. All pertinent bibliographical information has been retained. If the collection also contains unlisted or unsorted material, it has been noted in the box-and-folder list. This finding aid will be updated periodically to account for new materials that may be added to the collection. The date of the most recent update is noted on the title page. For information on material that may have been added since the last update, please consult the Public Service Desk.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This finding aid encompasses accessions of single manuscripts and small groups of manuscripts and other papers, by or pertaining to John Masefield, which the library has chosen to group and list as an artificial collection.

did.unittitle: I. Letters from John Masefield
TNS to Mrs. George Pierce Baker, Cirencester, [England]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift, 1945.

did.unittitle: AL to Mr. Christy, Oxford
TLS to Mrs. Davies, Abingdon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Removed from: A Macbeth Production by John Masefield (London, 1945). Purchase, 1977.

did.unittitle: ALS to Mrs. Goode, Cholsey, Berks

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Encloses poem, "Creed," holograph. Gift, 1963.

did.unittitle: ALS to Mr. Gosse, Cholsey, Berks
2 ALS to Geoffrey Handley-Taylor, Abingdon, Berks

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift, 1959.

did.unittitle: 6 ALS to Mrs. Thomas Hardy, Boars Hill, Oxford

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Most of the letters refer to Thomas Hardy. Gift, 1958.

did.unittitle: ANS to Mrs. [Oliver] Herford, Lollingdon, Cholsey, Berks

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Enclosing a manuscript copy of his poem, "August 1914," with an inscription to the Herfords and an original tail-piece drawing. Purchase, 1937.

did.unittitle: ALS to President Hamilton Holt of Rollins College, Abingdon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift, 1977.

did.unittitle: 111 ALS and 7 TLS to Joyce King

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by two holograph poems by Masefield; one holograph poem possibly by Masefield; holograph notes for the Malvern Festival; typescript of an article about King; two photographs of King; and one photograph of Masefield inscribed to King, 1952 Nov 15. Purchase, 1973.

did.unittitle: ALS to Mr. Marsh, Lollingdon, Cholsey, Berks

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by ALS to Mrs. Marsh, Congress Hotel and Annex, Chicago, 1918 Apr 10. Purchase, 1956.

did.unittitle: APcS to Elkin Mathews, 30, Maida Hill West, W.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Removed from: Ballads and Poems by John Masefield (1910).

did.unittitle: ALS to Elkin Mathews, 18 Porter Place, W.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Enclosing "A June Twilight" and "Midsummer Night," holograph poems. Accompanied by holograph note by Masefield; and clipping of "Roadways" and "To the Spring Flowers." Gift, 1992.

did.unittitle: ALS to Lady Mary Murray, Cholsey, Berks

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Removed from: The Battle of the Somme by John Masefield (London: William Heinemann, 1919).

did.unittitle: ALS to Mr. Neilson, Hill Crest, Boar's Hill, Oxford
ALS to [M. Moyca] Newell, Abingdon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Removed from: The Conway by John Masefield (London, 1953). Gift, 1962.

did.unittitle: 3 ALS and 1 APcS to C. K. Ogden

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Purchase, 1976.

did.unittitle: LS to F. C. Owlett in an unidentified hand

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift, 1943.

did.unittitle: ANS to William Lyon Phelps, New York City

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Removed from: Sonnets by John Masefield (New York, 1916). Accompanied by an article, including portrait of John Masefield, written by Edward Alden Jewell, which appeared in the New York Tribune , 1916 Jan 23. Gift, 1943.

did.unittitle: 21 ALS and 3 Christmas cards to Mrs. Edward Bliss Reed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To Elizabeth Reed. Accompanied by photograph of Masefield in army uniform. Gift, 1965.

did.unittitle: 2 ALS to William T. Reed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by manuscript leaf containing the private code used by Masefield and Reed. Gift, 1965.

did.unittitle: ALS to Sir William Watson, Oxford

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With envelope. Purchase, 1948.

did.unittitle: ALS to an unidentified recipient, London

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift, 1938.

did.unittitle: ALS to "Dear sir," Hill Crest, Boar's Hill, Oxford

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Removed from: The Widow in the Bye Street (London, 1912).

did.unittitle: ALS to "Dear Jasper," Boar's Hill, Oxford

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Removed from: Salt-Water Ballads by John Masefield (London: Grant Richards, 1902).

did.unittitle: Writings of John Masefield
[The gods have put this sorrow upon our souls...]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poem, holograph. Gift, 1994.

did.unittitle: "Ships"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Corrected galley proof of poem. Purchase, 1942.

did.unittitle: "Sonnet"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed version. Gift, 1994.

did.unittitle: [We fought in the grass [and] the uplands the whole of a summer's day...]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poem, holograph. Gift, 1994.

did.unittitle: Other
Color photograph of Sir John Lavery's portrait of Masefield, done around 1937

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original portrait is the property of Mr. Corliss Lamont, New York City. Gift.

did.unittitle: "John Masefield, O.M. The Queen's Poet Laureate: A Bibliography and Eighty- First Birthday Tribute" compiled by Geoffrey Handley-Taylor

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed prospectus. Accompanied by clipping. Gift, 1994.

did.unittitle: "Mr. John Masefield" by Ashley Gibson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Galley proof, corrected. Gift, 1994.

did.unittitle: Portrait of Masefield

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed by Masefield. Gift, 1944.

did.unittitle: Printed version of manuscript sheet music composed by Rolanda Ronal for Mansfield's poem, "A Hundred Years Ago"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift, 1959.

did.unittitle: Signature

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Second page of letter inscribed "With all best wishes to you." Removed from: Good Friday and Other Poems (New York: Macmillan, 1916). Gift, 1962.

did.unittitle: Manuscript leaf containing the private code used by Masefield and William Reed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Framed. Gift, 1965.

did.unittitle: Restricted Fragile Papers

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 3 contains Restricted fragile material. Reference surrogates have been substituted in the main files. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: Box 1, folder 21
Box 1, folder 24
Box 1, folder 38
Box 1, folder 39
